name: bash_tool
summary: Execute a shell command via `sh -c` in the agent's working directory and return its output.
The tool is constructed with a glob pattern; commands that do not match the pattern are rejected before execution.
constraints: The pattern is set at registration time (e.g. `git *`). A separate registration is needed for each unrelated command family.
Default per-command timeout is 120 000 ms; the model may request up to 600 000 ms via `timeout_ms`.
Whether the tool is treated as read-only is a registration-time choice — set by the operator, not the model.
anti_patterns: To find files by name — use `glob_tool`.
To edit a file — use `edit_file_tool` or `write_file_tool`.
cautions: IMPORTANT: do not invoke `grep`, `rg`, `find`, `ls`, `cat`, or `sed` here when a dedicated tool exists — those tools are faster, structured, and surface line numbers the model can cite.
IMPORTANT: the rejection is a hard fail, not a warning — if the command does not match the registered pattern, the tool returns an error and runs nothing.

IMPORTANT: side effects depend on the registered command family — a `git status` registration is read-only, a `git push` registration is destructive. Treat as destructive by default unless you know the registered pattern only matches read-only commands.
output: Plain text. The combined stdout and stderr of the command, trimmed.
On non-zero exit, an error message including the exit code. On timeout, an error message naming the elapsed time. On pattern mismatch, an error message naming the registered pattern.

command.description: The bash command to execute. Must match the registered glob pattern; otherwise the call fails before execution.

timeout_ms.description: Per-command timeout in milliseconds (default: 120000, max: 600000). The process is killed on timeout.
